Home/Atlas/Keywords/C++ Keywords

C++ Keywords

C++ has a set of reserved keywords that have special meaning in the language. These keywords cannot be used as identifiers (such as variable names, function names, etc.) and are fundamental to the syntax and structure of C++ programs.

List of C++ Keywords

#
^
  • alignas
  • alignof
  • and
  • and_eq
  • asm
  • auto
  • bitand
  • bitor
  • bool
  • break
  • case
  • catch
  • char
  • char16_t
  • char32_t
  • class
  • compl
  • const
  • constexpr
  • const_cast
  • continue
  • decltype
  • default
  • delete
  • do
  • double
  • dynamic_cast
  • else
  • enum
  • explicit
  • export
  • extern
  • for
  • friend
  • goto
  • if
  • inline
  • int
  • long
  • mutable
  • namespace
  • new
  • noexcept
  • not
  • not_eq
  • nullptr
  • operator
  • or
  • or_eq
  • private
  • protected
  • public
  • register
  • reinterpret_cast
  • return
  • short
  • signed
  • sizeof
  • static
  • static_assert
  • static_cast
  • struct
  • switch
  • template
  • this
  • thread_local
  • throw
  • true
  • try
  • typedef
  • typeid
  • typename
  • union
  • unsigned
  • using
  • virtual
  • void
  • volatile
  • wchar_t
  • while
  • xor
  • xor_eq
